message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): expired-budget cx does not hang (tick 378)

Added `distributed_http_client_with_expired_budget_does_not_hang` —
snapshot test pinning the observable shape of `DistributedHttpClient::get`
with a Cx whose `Budget::new().with_deadline(Time::ZERO)` means "budget
already exhausted at construction".

Budget-expired is a signal distinct from cx-cancel (pinned by tick 313):
- cx-cancel: "operator pulled the plug"
- budget-expired: "the time-box on this operation is over"

Observed behavior at HEAD (printed to stderr on test run):
  elapsed = 11.9ms
  outer_timeout_result = Ok(Err(HttpError(Io(Custom {
      kind: UnexpectedEof,
      error: "connection closed before response headers"
  }))))

The error is UnexpectedEof-shaped (not a budget-specific variant).
This suggests the asupersync HTTP client path doesn't observe cx
budget at its entry point — something downstream (likely the TCP
connect or first read attempt) fast-fails in some budget-aware way.
Without deeper instrumentation, the precise cause is out of scope
for this test.

What the test actually pins:
1. Bounded latency: < 15s (caught by the outer timeout safety net).
2. No panic on expired-budget cx.
3. The observable shape for the current commit is printed to stderr
   for anyone diffing against a future change.

This is a snapshot of current behavior, not a prescriptive contract.
If a future asupersync release adds explicit budget observation at
the HTTP client entry, this test will continue passing (and start
completing even faster) — the only failing mode is a regression that
broke all budget handling and caused a hang.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 22s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
